title: Two offers to chase, two different first rounds

mcq.question_md: Priya has a TCS campus drive on Monday and a Zoho off-campus test on Friday. Based on how these companies typically filter freshers in the first round, what should her week look like?
options: Same prep for both — all companies start with system design questions
Timed aptitude mocks for TCS; DSA problem practice for Zoho
DSA grinding for TCS; aptitude mocks for Zoho
Skip prep for both — first rounds are only resume shortlisting
mcq.explanation_md: Service companies like TCS filter the bulk of candidates with a timed **aptitude and verbal test** — speed under a clock eliminates more students there than anything technical, so timed mocks are the right prep. Product companies like Zoho filter with **coding rounds** — easy-to-medium DSA problems, often on an online judge. Option C is the tempting reversal; students who prepare the wrong game for each door lose winnable rounds. Option A fails because system design is a mid-level topic, rarely asked of freshers anywhere. And first rounds are almost never resume-only for freshers — both pipelines make you earn the interview with a test.
